duration_rule: 6 months or more (more or less daily for age 5 and under; weekly for older)

symptoms.description: The patient frequently loses their temper, is touchy or easily annoyed, and is often angry and resentful.
The patient frequently argues with authority figures or adults, and often actively defies or refuses to comply with rules.
The patient has been spiteful or vindictive at least twice within the past six months.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: At least 3 of 15 listed behaviors across four categories
duration_rule: 12 months (with at least 1 symptom in the past 6 months)

symptoms.description: The patient often bullies, threatens, or intimidates others.
The patient often initiates physical fights.
The patient has used a weapon that can cause serious physical harm to others (e.g., a bat, brick, broken bottle, knife, or gun).
The patient has been physically cruel to people.
The patient has been physically cruel to animals.
The patient has stolen while confronting a victim (e.g., mugging, purse snatching, extortion, or armed robbery).
The patient has forced someone into sexual activity.
The patient has deliberately engaged in fire setting with the intention of causing serious damage.
The patient has deliberately destroyed others' property by means other than fire setting.
The patient has broken into someone else's house, building, or car.
The patient often lies to obtain goods or favors or to avoid obligations (i.e., "cons" others).
The patient has stolen items of nontrivial value without confronting a victim (e.g., shoplifting without breaking and entering, or forgery).
The patient often stays out at night despite parental prohibitions, beginning before age 13 years.
The patient has run away from home overnight at least twice while living in the parental or parental surrogate home, or once without returning for a lengthy period.
The patient is often truant from school, beginning before age 13 years.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Conduct Disorder, with Limited Prosocial Emotions
threshold_count: At least 2 of 4 specific emotional features in addition to CD criteria

symptoms.description: The patient often bullies, threatens, or intimidates others.
The patient often initiates physical fights.
The patient has used a weapon that can cause serious physical harm to others (e.g., a bat, brick, broken bottle, knife, or gun).
The patient has been physically cruel to people.
The patient has been physically cruel to animals.
The patient has stolen while confronting a victim (e.g., mugging, purse snatching, extortion, or armed robbery).
The patient has forced someone into sexual activity.
The patient has deliberately engaged in fire setting with the intention of causing serious damage.
The patient has deliberately destroyed others' property by means other than fire setting.
The patient has broken into someone else's house, building, or car.
The patient often lies to obtain goods or favors or to avoid obligations (i.e., "cons" others).
The patient has stolen items of nontrivial value without confronting a victim (e.g., shoplifting without breaking and entering, or forgery).
The patient often stays out at night despite parental prohibitions, beginning before age 13 years.
The patient has run away from home overnight at least twice while living in the parental or parental surrogate home, or once without returning for a lengthy period.
The patient is often truant from school, beginning before age 13 years.
The patient lacks remorse, showing indifference to or actively rationalizing having hurt, mistreated, or stolen from another person.
The patient lacks empathy and is unwilling to recognize or identify with the feelings and needs of others.
The patient shows a lack of concern about their performance at school, work, or in other important activities.
The patient exhibits a shallow or deficient affect, demonstrating a limited expression of emotions.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Twice weekly aggression (no harm) OR 3 episodes causing harm/damage per year
duration_rule: 3 months for sub-destructive aggression; 1 year for destructive/harmful episodes

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits recurrent behavioral outbursts representing a failure to control aggressive impulses.
The patient exhibits aggressiveness during outbursts that is grossly out of proportion to any provocation or precipitating psychosocial stressor.
The patient experiences aggressive outbursts that are impulsive, not premeditated, and not committed to achieve a tangible objective.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.
The patient deliberately and purposefully sets multiple fires.
The patient feels tension or affective arousal before setting a fire, followed by a sense of release or pleasure afterward.
The patient exhibits a fascination with or attraction to fire and its situational contexts.
The patient experiences relief or pleasure when setting fires or witnessing their aftermath.

threshold_count: Repeatedly acting on impulses to steal unneeded objects

symptoms.description: The patient recurrently fails to resist impulses to steal objects that are not needed for personal use or for their monetary value.
The patient experiences an increasing sense of tension or excitement immediately before committing the theft.
The patient experiences pleasure, gratification, or relief at the time of committing the theft.
The patient exhibits recurrent behavioral outbursts representing a failure to control aggressive impulses.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Other Specified Disruptive, Impulse-Control, and Conduct Disorder
threshold_count: Clinically important symptoms that fall below threshold for defined disorders

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits clinically important disruptive, impulse-control, or conduct symptoms that fall below the diagnostic threshold for defined disorders.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Unspecified Disruptive, Impulse-Control, and Conduct Disorder
threshold_count: Disruptive behavior present but insufficient information for a specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits disruptive behavior, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.
